data.description: When you move, you can move half your speed in the specified direction. For example, if your speed is 30 feet, you could move 15 feet in a straight line. If you have no movement or your speed is 0, you can't move. To move while prone, you must crawl or use magic such as teleportation. Every foot of crawling costs an extra difficult foot due to the awkwardness of the terrain.
Each creature takes up different amounts of space. The Categories table shows how much space a particular size category controls.
Each foot of movement costs 1 action.

You can move through a nonhostile creature’s space. In contrast, you can move through a hostile creature’s space only if the creature is at least two sizes larger than you or is Tiny and you are at least Medium. Remember that another creature's space is difficult terrain for you. Whether a creature is a friend or an enemy, it won’t willingly let you move through its space.
The space a creature occupies and moves through.
